summary: Re-review of `tools/anatomy-extension/` confirms both previously blocking `content.js` fixes landed correctly: the dead `/edit` submit path was replaced with an explicit deprecation banner and link to canvas-engine on `:3050`, and `proxyFetch()` now computes `credentials='include'` only for same-origin asset requests while leaving cross-origin requests at `'omit'`, which the background worker honors. I did not find any new issues introduced by those patches. The five previously deferred follow-up items are still present; I’ve listed them below for tracking, but per instruction they were notpen

issues.description: Deferred, not counted in score: `doImport()` still accepts `anatomy-v1` and legacy `labels` payloads with no structural validation beyond a top-level array check, then pushes entries directly into live state. Malformed selectors/layers/notes can still propagate into later rendering/export paths.
issues.suggested_fix: Validate each imported record against a strict schema before `normalize()`: require bounded strings for `label`/`selector`/`layer`, reject unknown keys, cap array sizes, and drop invalid entries with a user-visible summary.

issues.description: Deferred, not counted in score: imported `r.layer` values are still interpolated raw into exported map HTML attributes (`data-layer="${r.layer}"`) for both mockup boxes and callouts. Because `doImport()` preserves arbitrary `layer` strings, a crafted import can still reach an HTML injection sink.
issues.suggested_fix: Constrain `layer` to the known enum (`ui|api|ext|lib|state`) during import/normalization and HTML-escape any remaining attribute interpolations in export rendering.

issues.description: Deferred, not counted in score: the extension still requests broader privileges than the reviewed features need (`tabs`, `alarms`, and `<all_urls>` host access across both content scripts).
issues.suggested_fix: Reduce permissions to the minimum required set, narrow match patterns/host permissions, and gate dev-only capabilities behind a separate development manifest or build flag.

issues.description: Deferred, not counted in score: manifest metadata still drifts from the actual versioned behavior. The package version is `0.3.15`, while the description still advertises `v0.3.3` features.
issues.suggested_fix: Update the description to match the current release or remove embedded version callouts from user-facing metadata.

issues.description: Deferred, not counted in score: the dev hot-reload alarm still polls `localhost:8088/dev/version` every 30 seconds in the shipped service worker.
issues.suggested_fix: Move the polling loop behind a dev-only build flag, or disable alarm registration outside explicit development mode.

strengths: Verified the removed `/edit` POST path no longer attempts to call the deleted `localhost:8088/edit` endpoint; the modal now surfaces a deprecation message and safe `canvas-engine` handoff instead.
Verified `proxyFetch()` now derives credentials from URL origin and passes that decision through `anatomy:fetch`, while `background.js` honors only `include` or `omit` accordingly.
No new correctness or security regressions were introduced in the touched `content.js` paths during this patch set review.
